aegis_export.flawed_summary: Female patient, 54 years old, with recurrent tension headache and migraine history. No known allergies (NKDA). Medication: amitriptyline 25mg. Plan: paracetamol during crisis, continue amitriptyline, neurology referral.
aegis_export.corrected_summary: Female patient, 54 years old, with recurrent tension headache (diagnosis: tension headache; rule out migraine without aura) and migraine history diagnosed 10 years ago. ALLERGY STATUS DISCREPANCY — PATIENT SAFETY ALERT: Historical record states No Known Drug Allergies (NKDA); however, same-day nursing note documents a patient-reported ibuprofen allergy with a prior adverse reaction (skin rash) documented in the emergency department 6 months ago — not yet updated in the historical record. This represents an active patient safety discrepancy requiring urgent reconciliation. Medication: amitriptyline 25mg at night (migraine prophylaxis). Plan: paracetamol 1g q8h during crisis, continue amitriptyline, AVOID NSAIDs until allergy status is clarified, neurology referral for evaluation, and pending allergy record update with clinical pharmacy to reconcile conflicting allergy information.
